runtime_authority: connected backend version and advertised effective capabilities
policy_authority: reviewed public source linked from each document

documents.title: Start with the job you need to do
documents.summary: Mobazha is an open commerce stack for running, using, and extending independent marketplaces and stores.

documents.title: Choose hosted or self-hosted Mobazha
documents.summary: Choose the operating model from control, responsibility, integrations, and service needs—not from a false free-versus-paid shortcut.
documents.source_label: Mobazha public product and release boundaries

documents.title: Use Mobazha documentation as an Agent
documents.summary: Resolve authority, applicability, and user consent before turning documentation into an action.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Sell through a Mobazha store
documents.summary: A practical map from store setup to settlement, with costs and responsibilities visible before an order is accepted.

documents.title: Buy with inspectable terms
documents.summary: Confirm the seller, item, delivery terms, payment route, and complete cost breakdown before committing funds.

documents.title: Operate your own Mobazha Node
documents.summary: Self-hosting keeps store data and operations under the operator's control and does not require an optional managed-service subscription.

documents.title: Install a Mobazha Node
documents.summary: Build the release candidate from public source, start it locally, and verify the UI and health boundary before exposing it.

documents.title: Configure a self-hosted node
documents.summary: Make deployment mode, data location, network exposure, payments, and external dependencies explicit and recoverable.
documents.source_label: Mobazha Node command and deployment sources

documents.title: Back up and upgrade safely
documents.summary: Treat upgrade readiness as a recoverability exercise, not only a package update.

documents.title: Secure a self-hosted node
documents.summary: Protect the host, administrative boundary, signing material, payment integrations, backups, and recovery path as one system.
documents.source_label: Mobazha security policy and extension security model

documents.title: Troubleshoot a Mobazha Node
documents.summary: Diagnose version, process, health, capability, configuration, and dependency failures without exposing sensitive data.

documents.title: Connect optional hosted capabilities
documents.summary: Keep local node ownership separate from any optional account or hosted service connection.
documents.applies_to: Design direction; not a shipped guarantee

documents.title: Build on advertised capabilities
documents.summary: Integrations should discover what a backend supports instead of assuming every Mobazha deployment exposes the same surface.

documents.title: Runtime capabilities and product composition
documents.summary: Discover effective behavior from the connected backend and keep deployment, experience, capabilities, permissions, and experiments separate.
documents.source_label: Mobazha compatibility and Unified runtime contracts

documents.title: HTTP API and OpenAPI
documents.summary: Use the generated Mobazha Node OpenAPI contract as the operation and schema reference, then verify runtime capabilities before calling optional features.

documents.title: WebSocket events
documents.summary: Use authenticated WebSocket events for timely UI updates, then reconcile important state through the authoritative HTTP API.
documents.source_label: Mobazha WebSocket gateway and event registry

documents.title: MCP and agent integrations
documents.summary: Agents can discover and invoke permitted commerce capabilities, but cannot replace user consent, policy, or backend authorization.

documents.title: Webhook integration contract
documents.summary: Design consumers for authentication, retries, duplication, reordering, and version change before depending on event delivery.
documents.source_label: Mobazha webhook engine and OpenAPI contract

documents.title: Extend Mobazha through public contracts
documents.summary: Choose the narrowest typed extension mechanism and preserve Core authority, capability gates, isolation, recovery, and audit.
documents.applies_to: Design direction; not a shipped guarantee

documents.title: Reference and source map
documents.summary: Find the repository that owns a fact before proposing or automating a change.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Understand the Mobazha project
documents.summary: Separate what ships now from project principles, reviewed policy, historical proposals, and long-term direction.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Architecture and trust boundaries
documents.summary: Mobazha separates user clients, independently operated backends, optional hosted services, and external payment or delivery systems.

documents.title: Founding whitepaper
documents.summary: A public-review account of Mobazha's problem framing, trust model, human-and-Agent interfaces, and approach to sustainable open commerce.
documents.applies_to: Design direction; not a shipped guarantee
documents.source_label: Public-review edition prepared for the Mobazha project

documents.title: Fees and sustainable economics
documents.summary: Mobazha does not define one unavoidable commission for every transaction. Each actual charge must identify its owner, purpose, basis, and point of consent.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Project security model
documents.summary: Security depends on explicit authority, fail-closed capabilities, protected signing material, hostile-input assumptions, and private disclosure.

documents.title: Legal, privacy, license, and trademark boundaries
documents.summary: Separate software licenses, hosted-service terms, privacy commitments, third-party obligations, and trademark rights.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface
documents.source_label: Mobazha public policies and repository notices

documents.title: Release scope and maturity
documents.summary: The documentation distinguishes current release-candidate behavior from previews and future design.

documents.title: Documentation and policy governance
documents.summary: Important project claims need an owner, source, review date, status, and visible change path.
documents.applies_to: Design direction; not a shipped guarantee
documents.source_label: Mobazha public governance and documentation policy

documents.title: Public decisions and proposals
documents.summary: Use RFCs for changes still being evaluated, ADRs for decisions already made, and history records for superseded public material.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Requests for Comment
documents.summary: Review substantial protocol, policy, economic, security, governance, and cross-repository proposals before treating them as commitments.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Architecture Decision Records
documents.summary: Preserve why durable technical and product decisions were made, what alternatives were rejected, and what would supersede them.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: History and supersession
documents.summary: Preserve replaced public proposals and guidance without allowing historical text to masquerade as current behavior or policy.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Releases
documents.summary: Use repository releases for exact versions, migration notes, checksums, and known issues.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Contribute to Mobazha
documents.summary: Choose the owning repository, agree on large changes early, preserve public contracts, add tests, update documentation, and sign off commits.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Get help and report problems
documents.summary: Use public support for reproducible product questions, repository issues for defects, and private reporting for security problems.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: 从你要完成的任务开始
documents.summary: Mobazha 是一套开放商业技术栈，可用于自行托管商店、托管服务、市场、结账和 Agent 自动化。

documents.title: 选择托管服务或自行托管
documents.summary: 根据控制权、运维责任、集成需求和服务价值选择，而不是把它简化成“免费或收费”。
documents.source_label: Mobazha 公开产品与发布边界

documents.title: Agent 如何使用 Mobazha 文档
documents.summary: 在把文档转化为操作之前，先确认权威来源、适用版本、身份、权限和用户同意。
documents.applies_to: 当前公开项目政策或服务界面

documents.title: 通过 Mobazha 开店与销售
documents.summary: 从店铺准备、商品发布、报价与付款，到订单履约和售后，保持责任与成本透明。
documents.source_label: Mobazha Node 与 Unified 公开实现

documents.title: 在条款与成本透明的前提下购买
documents.summary: 付款前确认卖家、商品、配送、支付路径、完整成本和争议规则。

documents.title: 运行自己的 Mobazha Node
documents.summary: 自行托管让店铺数据与运营处于你的控制之下，同时把安全、备份、升级和可用性责任交给你。
documents.source_label: Mobazha Node 公开部署源码

documents.title: 安装 Mobazha Node
documents.summary: 从公开源码构建候选版本，在本地和测试网启动，并在对外暴露前验证 UI 与运行边界。

documents.title: 基于后端实际公开的能力进行开发
documents.summary: 集成应发现当前后端支持什么，而不是假设每种 Mobazha 部署都公开相同能力。

documents.title: 运行时能力与产品组合
documents.summary: 从后端发现有效行为，并把部署方式、产品体验、能力、权限和实验开关分开处理。
documents.source_label: Mobazha 兼容性与 Unified 运行时契约

documents.title: Mobazha 创始白皮书
documents.summary: 关于问题定义、信任模型、人类与 Agent 接口，以及开放商业可持续路径的公开评审版。
documents.applies_to: 设计方向，不代表已交付承诺
documents.source_label: Mobazha 白皮书中文维护译本

documents.title: 收费与可持续经济模式
documents.summary: Mobazha 不为所有交易定义一个无法避免的统一抽成；实际收费必须说明服务、收款方、依据和确认点。
documents.applies_to: 当前公开项目政策或服务界面
documents.source_label: 收费与付费服务公开政策

documents.title: 获取帮助与报告问题
documents.summary: 产品问题使用公开支持，缺陷提交到对应仓库，安全问题必须私下报告。
documents.applies_to: 当前公开项目政策或服务界面
